contribution.title: Chandrasekhar limit
contribution.description: Showed that white dwarfs cannot exceed approximately 1.4 solar masses without collapsing; predicted the existence of objects beyond degeneracy pressure support.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Anchors stellar-mass black hole formation pathways.

contribution.title: The Mathematical Theory of Black Holes
contribution.description: Definitive monograph on the perturbation theory of black holes (Schwarzschild, Reissner-Nordstrom, Kerr).
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Primary analytic reference for ISCO, photon sphere, quasi-normal modes, and perturbation methods.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Push analytic methods (separation of variables, special functions, perturbation theory) as far as they will go before resorting to numerics.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: Does this problem admit a separable form?
What does perturbation theory tell us about stability?
What is the full hierarchy of governing equations?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Sometimes pushing analytic methods past the point where numerics would be faster and clearer.

reference.citation: Chandrasekhar, S. (1983). The Mathematical Theory of Black Holes. Oxford University Press.
Chandrasekhar, S. (1931). The maximum mass of ideal white dwarfs. Astrophys. J. 74, 81.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Implementing perturbation theory, quasi-normal modes, ringdown, or anything that admits a separable analytic form.
how_to_apply.cautions: His notation is dense. Translate carefully to modern conventions when implementing.
